decisions.decision: When .cnogo/.venv is missing, fall through to current-interpreter indexing instead of returning early. Remove the hard-skip block at line 545.
decisions.rationale: The venv is a performance/isolation optimization, not a hard requirement. ContextGraph imports and indexes fine from any Python that has kuzu available. The current early return makes the hook a no-op in fresh repos and test environments.

decisions.decision: Reorder the venv logic: (1) if venv exists and we're not in it, delegate to venv python (existing behavior). (2) Otherwise fall through to direct import and index. Remove the venv-missing early return entirely.
decisions.rationale: Simplest change. The existing try/except around the import/index block already handles ImportError gracefully (prints warning, returns 0). No new error handling needed.

decisions.decision: Keep the informational stderr message about missing venv but downgrade from blocking skip to advisory warning. Print it before attempting fallback indexing.
decisions.rationale: Users should know the venv is recommended for best results (tree-sitter parsing), but indexing should still attempt to work without it.

constraints: Hook must return 0 on all failure paths (non-blocking constraint)
Must not change behavior when venv IS present (delegation path unchanged)
Existing try/except at line 567 catches ImportError if kuzu is unavailable

priorWork.description: Previous fix improved the warning message but kept the hard-skip. This fix removes the skip entirely.
